texto: Good choice,' Laura Said. 'Our neighbour, Mrs Crabtree, came round this morning and she put it best. Her theory is that fame is like a bubble. It looks gorgeous on the outside, as if it's been painted with pretty colours, but when you pop it there's nothing there. She said that life, love and friendship are what matters, and that what you do is more important than what you show.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Lauren St. John: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “Good choice,' Laura Said. 'Our neighbour, Mrs Crabtree, came round this morning and she put it be…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amizade, fame, reflexao pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de bom-dia e termino.

frase_original: Good choice,' Laura Said. 'Our neighbour, Mrs Crabtree, came round this morning and she put it best. Her theory is that fame is like a bubble. It looks gorgeous on the outside, as if it's been painted with pretty colours, but when you pop it there's nothing there. She said that life, love and friendship are what matters, and that what you do is more important than what you show.
